phrases.text_local: Πού βρίσκεται ακριβώς ο ανδριάντας του Καποδίστρια;
phrases.cultural_note: In Greece, public statues of historical figures are typically located in central squares or along pedestrian zones. Directing to an exact spot without clear signage is avoided to prevent misinformation.

likely_response.local: Είναι στην κεντρική πλατεία, κοντά στον πεζόδρομο.
likely_response.translation_en: It is in the central square, near the pedestrian street.

responses.english: It is in the central square, near the pedestrian street.
responses.french: Είναι στην κεντρική πλατεία, κοντά στον πεζόδρομο.

phrases.text_local: Πού είναι το πιο κοντινό ΑΤΜ;
phrases.cultural_note: ATM locations in pedestrian zones are often near central points like squares or walkways. Exact placement without visible signage cannot be guaranteed.

likely_response.local: Το πιο κοντινό είναι λίγο πιο κάτω, δίπλα στην πλατεία.
likely_response.translation_en: The nearest one is a little further down, next to the square.

responses.english: The nearest one is a little further down, next to the square.
responses.french: Το πιο κοντινό είναι λίγο πιο κάτω, δίπλα στην πλατεία.

phrases.intent_en: Can I haggle at souvenir shops here?
phrases.text_local: Μπορώ να κάνω παζάρι στα μαγαζιά με σουβενίρ;
phrases.cultural_note: In Greek markets, haggling is not standard practice unless explicitly offered by the seller. Customers are expected to inquire directly with the shop owner.
